steps.title: Spot the two independent clauses
steps.content: The blank separates a complete thought about what scientists previously believed from another complete thought: “the Lund team's finding suggests otherwise.”

steps.title: Apply the independent clause joining rule
steps.content: A comma plus a coordinating conjunction correctly joins two independent clauses. Choice B provides both with “time, but,” using the conjunction “but” to mark the contrast between the previous belief and the new finding. A lacks the comma, C lacks the conjunction (leaving a comma splice), and D lacks both punctuation and conjunction (creating a run-on). That matches choice B — B is correct.
